purpose: Compile current work into a bounded operational packet so the agent can work from stable state instead of raw session turbulence.

current_objective.description: Current work objective in plain language.

working_set.description: Optional files, directories, or named surfaces currently believed to be in play.

known_blockers.description: Explicit blockers already known to the agent or user.

recent_residue.description: Short recent facts, interruptions, or unfinished observations that should survive the current turn.

preferred_lane.description: Optional preferred lane if the user already knows the work should stay in governance, gov2gov, 1project, narrative, or triage.

active_lane.description: Best-fit working lane for the current objective.

next_required_action.description: Single next action that should move the work forward.

ordered_remaining_steps.description: Short ordered sequence for continuing the work from the compiled state.

stop_point.description: Best current continuation anchor if the work stops here.

evidence_status.description: How strongly the compiled state is anchored to observable workspace context or residue.

session_degradation_signals.description: Signals that the rider/horse relationship is degrading into drift, negation, or pseudo-state.

working_surface_presence.description: Present and missing path-like surfaces from the declared working set.

measurement_basis.description: What the compiler used to ground the packet.

result_example.active_objective: materialize current governance work without trusting copied schema presence
result_example.stop_point: resume from declared working surfaces and current residue
